description: New hospital ward, clinical facility, or hospital reconfiguration. Covers acute, community, and mental health settings.

typical_total_range.note: Single ward to major reconfiguration. New hospitals typically GBP 200-600m.

typical_benefits.description: QALYs gained from improved care quality, reduced waiting times, avoided adverse events
typical_benefits.monetisation: QALY value (GBP 70,000 per Green Book supplementary guidance). Use 1.5% health discount rate (Green Book 2026).

typical_benefits.description: Better primary/community care reduces acute admissions
Modern facilities improve clinical workflow and reduce agency staff costs
If reconfiguration changes travel distances for patients/visitors

default_parameters.health_discount_rate_note: Green Book 2026 mandates 1.5% for health/QALY benefits (not the standard 3.5%)
default_parameters.additionality_note: NHS capital projects are not subject to standard additionality adjustments (no market displacement in healthcare delivery)

reference_bcr_range.note: Highly variable. Emergency department expansions and critical infrastructure replacement can have very high BCRs. Elective care expansions lower. Reference: NHS England SOC/OBC published business cases.
reference_cases: Midland Metropolitan University Hospital OBC (2015): BCR 2.1
Royal Liverpool University Hospital FBC (2013): BCR 1.8

typical_risks: Clinical adjacency requirements constraining design options
Decanting/phasing costs if building on an operational hospital site
Medical equipment specification changes during long build programmes
NHS workforce availability (recruitment to new facility)
Cost inflation specific to healthcare construction (above general construction indices)

publications: DHSC Capital Regime and Investment Business Case Guide
